creative_brief: Split-screen visual: left side shows a long, grey highway with refrigerated trucks stretching into the distance, desaturated and industrial. Right side shows a close-up of rich, deep-red grass-fed beef on butcher paper with a handwritten farm name tag from Kempsey, NSW. Warm, natural light on the right.
primary_text:
2,400km. That's the average distance supermarket beef travels before it reaches the shelf.

Then it sits in cold storage for 14 to 21 days.

At FarmThru, our 100% grass-fed beef comes from regenerative farms like Rachel Ward's in Kempsey. It's days old when you collect it. Not weeks.

No hormones. No antibiotics. No feedlot finishing. Just cattle raised on pasture, with a short trip to our hub.

Collect from Brookvale, Monday to Friday. No lock-in. Order when you want.
headline: 2,400km vs a short drive to Brookvale
description: 100% grass-fed beef, days old when you collect. From regenerative farms you can actually name.
